'''Matt Diskin''' (born 27 January 1982) an English professional [[rugby league]] coach was the [[head coach]] of [[Oldham RLFC|Oldham]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|hk}} in the 2000s and 2010s.

He played at representative level for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]], [[England national rugby league team|England]] and [[Yorkshire rugby league team|Yorkshire]], and at club level for [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] and [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]]<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> and coached at club level for the [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] and [[Batley Bulldogs|Batley]].<ref name="Coach Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> Diskin played for the [[Leeds Rhinos]] for 10-years, and won four [[Super League]] championships with the club in 2004 ([[Super League IX]]), 2007 ([[Super League XII]]), 2008 ([[Super League XIII]]), and 2009 ([[Super League XIV]]), before moving to [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] for the 2011 season, he remained with the Bradford club until the end of his playing career in 2014.  He then became head coach of the [[Batley Bulldogs|Batley]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Background==
Diskin was born in [[Dewsbury]], [[West Yorkshire]], England.

==Playing career==
Diskin is a product of amateur rugby league club [[Dewsbury Moor]] ARLFC and growing up he attended St. John Fisher High School in Dewsbury, as did [[Francis Cummins]] and [[Ryan Sheridan (rugby league)|Ryan Sheridan]].

===Leeds Rhinos===
Diskin made his début first-team appearance for [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] in 2001 against [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]]. He played for the [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] at {{rlp|HK}} and scored a [[Try (rugby)|try]] in their [[2004 Super League Grand Final]] victory against [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]], and was man of the match.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rhinos-nostalgia-furner-bows-out-grand-final-victory-over-bulls-2004-242879|title = Leeds Rhinos Nostalgia: Furner bows out with Grand Final victory over Bulls in 2004}}</ref> He was selected to represent [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] in the [[2004 Tri-nations]] tournament.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2004/nov/19/rugbyleague.andywilson|title = Rugby league: Tri-Nations series: Debutant Diskin has final chance|website = [[TheGuardian.com]]|date = 19 November 2004}}</ref>

Diskin played for Leeds in the [[2005 Challenge Cup]] Final at {{rlp|HK}} in their loss against [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]].<ref>{{cite news|title=Hull stun Leeds in Challenge Cup|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/challenge_cup/4169230.stm|access-date=17 August 2013|work=[[BBC Sport]]|date=27 August 2005}}</ref> He played for [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] from the interchange bench in their [[2005 Super League Grand Final]] loss against [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]]. He played in the [[2008 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]].<ref name="2008 Grand Final">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7642170.stm|title=2008 Grand Final|publisher=BBC|work=BBC Sport|date=4 October 2008|access-date=5 October 2008}}</ref>

He played in the [[2009 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at [[Old Trafford]], scoring a try in the record-breaking third consecutive Grand Final victory over St Helens.<ref>{{cite news|last=AFP|title=Leeds makes it Super League hat-trick|url=http://www.abc.net.au/news/2009-10-11/leeds-makes-it-super-league-hat-trick/1098564|access-date=22 November 2019|newspaper=[[Australian Broadcasting Corporation|ABC]] News|date=11 October 2009}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last=AAP|author-link=Australian Associated Press|title=Leeds claim third successive Grand Final|url=http://www.nrl.com/NewsViews/LatestNews/NewsArticle/tabid/10874/newsId/57077/Default.aspx|access-date=22 November 2019|newspaper=[[National Rugby League|nrl]].com|date=11 October 2009|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121003225647/http://www.nrl.com/NewsViews/LatestNews/NewsArticle/tabid/10874/newsId/57077/Default.aspx|archive-date=3 October 2012|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last=Fletcher|first=Paul|title=St Helens 10–18 Leeds Rhinos|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/8290503.stm|access-date=22 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=10 October 2009}}</ref>

2010 saw Diskin celebrate his testimonial year at Leeds after ten years of service to the most successful club of recent years. Diskin had been instrumental in all of Leeds' championship winning campaigns.  He played in the [[2010 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Leeds 6-30 Warrington">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/8938067.stm|title=Leeds 6–30 Warrington|publisher=BBC|date=28 August 2010|access-date=22 November 2019|first=Ged|last=Scott}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2010/aug/28/leeds-rhinos-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final |location=London |work=The Guardian |title=Chris Hicks hat-trick leads Warrington to Challenge Cup triumph over Leeds |first=Andy |last=Wilson |date=28 August 2010 |access-date=22 November 2019<!--DASHBot--> |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100831105934/http://www.guardian.co.uk/sport/2010/aug/28/leeds-rhinos-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final |archive-date=31 August 2010 |url-status=live  }}</ref>

On 19 October 2010, it was announced that Diskin had agreed a move to [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] on a three-year contract, and was expected to push for the starting {{rlp|hk}} position in the Bradford  line-up.

===Bradford Bulls===
Diskin appeared in two of 2011's four pre-season games. He played against [[Dewsbury Rams|Dewsbury]] and [[Wakefield Trinity]]. Diskin featured in 23 consecutive games from Round 1 ([[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]]) to Round 23 ([[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]]). He also appeared in the [[Challenge Cup]] game against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]]. He scored against [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]], [[Crusaders Rugby League|Crusaders]] and [[London Broncos|Harlequins RL]]. A shoulder injury kept him out for the rest of the season.

Diskin and [[Heath L'Estrange]] were appointed joint captains for the 2012 season. Diskin featured in two of the four pre-season games. He played against [[Dewsbury Rams|Dewsbury]] and [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]]. Diskin featured in 15 consecutive games from Round 1 ([[Catalans Dragons]]) to Round 15 ([[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]]). Diskin missed Rounds 16–23 due to injury. Diskin returned for Round 24 ([[Hull Kingston Rovers]]) to Round 27 ([[Catalans Dragons]]). He also featured in the [[Challenge Cup]] against [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] and [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]]. Diskin scored tries against [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] (1 try), [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] (1 try), [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] (2 tries), [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] (1 try) and [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] (1 try).

Diskin featured in 2013's pre-season friendlies against [[Dewsbury Rams|Dewsbury]] and [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]]. He scored against [[Dewsbury Rams|Dewsbury]] (1 try). He featured in four consecutive games from Round 1 ([[Wakefield Trinity]]) to Round 4 ([[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]]). He missed Round 5 due to injury. He returned for Round 6 ([[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]]) but was ill for Round 7. Diskin returned for Round 8 ([[Catalans Dragons]]) to Round 9 ([[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]]). He missed Round 10 due to an injury. Diskin featured in Round 11 ([[London Broncos]]) to Round 16 ([[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]]). He returned to the team for Round 18 ([[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]]) to Round 27 ([[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]]). Diskin also appeared in the [[Challenge Cup]] against [[London Broncos]]. Matt scored against [[Widnes Vikings]] (1 try), [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] (1 try) and [[Hull F.C.]] (1 try). Diskin signed a one-year extension to his contract midway through the season.

Diskin missed the pre-season friendlies against [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]] and [[Dewsbury Rams|Dewsbury]] due to injury. However he did feature in the friendly against [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]]. He featured in Round 1 ([[Castleford Tigers]]) to Round 6 ([[Hull Kingston Rovers]]). Diskin next featured in Round 8 ([[Salford Red Devils]]) then in Round 11 ([[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]]) to Round 13 ([[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]]). He also played in Round 15 ([[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]]) to Round 19 ([[Catalans Dragons]]). Diskin featured in Round 5 ([[Catalans Dragons]]) to the quarter-final ([[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]]) in the [[Challenge Cup]]. He scored against [[Hull F.C.]] (1 try).

==Coaching career==
===Bradford Bulls===
Diskin was put in temporary charge of the Bradford outfit on 16 June 2014 as a player coach. It was the day following the sacking of head coach [[Francis Cummins]], and assistant [[Lee St Hilaire]]. Diskin took charge of Bradford's home match against Hull Kingston Rovers where they lost 44–18 the Tuesday after [[James Lowes]] was announced as Bradford's new head coach.

At the end of the 2014 season, Diskin announced his retirement.<ref>{{cite news|title=Matt Diskin: Bradford Bulls' former Great Britain hooker to retire|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/29031422|work=BBC Sport|access-date=11 February 2015|date=3 September 2014}}</ref>

===Batley Bulldogs===
In April 2016 it was announced Diskin was to take over at [[Batley Bulldogs|Batley]] from 2017 after [[John Kear]] announced he would be joining [[Wakefield Trinity]] as director of rugby in 2017.{{citation needed|date=March 2024}}

===Oldham===
On 10 October 2019 Diskin was appointed as Head Coach.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/oldham-appoint-matt-diskin-as-new-head-coach/|title=Oldham appoint Matt Diskin as new head coach|date=10 October 2019|access-date=20 December 2020|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>

On 29 June 2021, Oldham sacked him after 9 defeats in the last 10 games with assistant-coach Brendan Sheridan taking over training.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=29 June 2021|accessdate=29 June 2021|title=Oldham part ways with head coach Matt Diskin|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/oldham-part-ways-with-head-coach-matt-diskin/}}</ref>
